config_rationale: Selected UCB with beta=2.0 over LogEI for the ligand exploration phase. Rationale: (a) The campaign has stagnated at 89.17% for 6 iterations with XPhos, confirming Rule R13 that stronger exploration is needed; (b) We are now testing hypotheses H1-H5 involving new ligands (BrettPhos, RuPhos, SPhos) where the GP has high uncertainty; (c) UCB's explicit exploration control (beta=2.0) prioritizes uncertain high-potential regions over greedy improvement, appropriate for breaking the 90% barrier; (d) LogEI would over-exploit the known XPhos optimum rather than explore BrettPhos/RuPhos where the true optimum may lie per H1-H2. Beta=2.0 provides ~95% confidence interval exploration, aggressive enough for the remaining 27 iterations but not so high as to waste budget on clearly inferior regions.
kernel_rationale: Matern-5/2 is the safe general-purpose default for Bayesian optimization with 13 observations and 26-dimensional one-hot encoding. It provides appropriate smoothness assumptions for chemical reaction yield surfaces without overfitting. The campaign has established a clear optimum (89.17% at Cs+/XPhos/DMF/0.057M/105°C) and now transitions to ligand exploration; Matern-5/2 balances exploitation of known good regions with exploration of new ligands (BrettPhos, RuPhos, SPhos) without the complexity of mixed kernels that require more data to fit interaction structures reliably.

config_rationale: Log Expected Improvement is the default stable choice for single-objective BO [KB:af_catalog]. With 15 iterations remaining and a known optimum at 89.17%, we need reliable improvement-based selection. UCB was tried in version 2 without resolving the model fitting issues. LogEI provides better numerical stability than classic EI and does not require tuning a beta parameter like UCB. Given the surrogate has been failing, we need an acquisition function that is robust to model uncertainty - LogEI's log-space transformation provides this. We are in exploitation phase (best=89.17% found), but given recent catastrophic failures (4.74%, 0%, 0.07%), we need the model to guide us back to high-yielding regions, which LogEI will do by exploiting regions near the current best while still exploring uncertain high-potential areas.
kernel_rationale: The campaign has 10 consecutive ModelFittingErrors with standard Matern-5/2 kernel on one-hot encoding. The mixed_sum_product kernel is specifically designed for encoded mixed spaces with uncertain interaction structure [KB:kernel_catalog]. It provides a weighted blend of additive (main effects) and multiplicative (interaction) behavior, which is critical since the data shows strong cation-ligand interaction effects (Cs+/XPhos=89%, Cs+/BrettPhos=42%, K+/BrettPhos=79%) that violate simple additive models. This kernel was explicitly recommended in the last reconfiguration attempt but may not have been properly applied. The one-hot encoding (26D) with 5 categorical variables creates a space where main effects and interactions are both present; mixed_sum_product is the only kernel option that explicitly balances these. If this fails, we will need to abandon one-hot encoding entirely for SMKBO with molecular fingerprints.
